channel.title: Tohoku on Hidden Japan Tokyo
channel.description: Recent content in Tohoku on Hidden Japan Tokyo

item.title: Beyond the Shinkansen Stop: Aomori
item.description: Most travelers pass straight through Shin-Aomori without stopping. Here's why that's a mistake — and what's waiting if you get off the train.

item.title: Japan's Greatest Fireworks Festivals: 4 Shows Worth Planning Your Trip Around
item.description: Four of Japan's most spectacular fireworks festivals — where the scale, artistry, and sheer physical impact turn a summer evening into something you won't forget.

item.title: Hidden Castle Towns Near Tokyo: 5 Day Trips Off the Tourist Trail
item.description: Five feudal castle towns within day-trip distance of Tokyo — from Little Edo Kawagoe to the samurai streets of Aizu-Wakamatsu. No rental car needed.

item.title: Japan Lighthouse Guide: 7 Hidden Coastal Gems Along Honshu Worth Visiting
item.description: Honshu's most scenic and least-visited lighthouses — from Chiba's 1874 Inubosaki to Shimane's tallest stone tower. All reachable without a car.

item.title: Hidden Temples in Tohoku: 5 Shrines Worth the Trip North
item.description: Five of Tohoku's most atmospheric temples and shrines — from Yamadera's clifftop halls to the 1,400-year-old pilgrimage at Dewa Sanzan. Crowd-free and remarkable.

item.title: Hidden Waterfalls in Japan: 5 Rural Gems Most Tourists Never Find
item.description: Five of Japan's most beautiful hidden waterfalls — tucked into rural valleys and mountain forests far from the tour group circuit. With access tips for each.

item.title: Rural Japan Food: 5 Local Food Experiences Worth Leaving Tokyo For
item.description: Five authentic rural Japanese food experiences you won't find in city restaurants — from Tohoku mountain cuisine to hand-pulled soba in a converted farmhouse.

item.title: Japan's Most Scenic Local Train Rides: 5 Routes Worth the Detour
item.description: Five of Japan's most beautiful local train journeys — coastal cliff lines, highland valleys, and routes where you might be the only foreign passenger on board.

item.title: 5 Hidden Onsen Towns in Japan Away from the Crowds (2026 Guide)
item.description: Five off-the-radar hot spring towns in Japan — including a free riverside rotemburo open 24/7 and a century-old ryokan deep in an Aomori beech forest.
